title: Harnessing Claude Agent SDK: Automate Your Bug Triage
hook: The most tedious part of my week used to be manually triaging bugs in Sentry—until I built a custom harness with Claude Agent SDK.
partner_takeaway: You can use Claude Agent SDK to automate bug triage workflows, saving time and reducing repetitive prompts.
reasoning: Concrete automation patterns are in demand among practitioners. This post delivers a tactical workflow that readers can replicate immediately, backed by a high-relevance external signal.

title: Headless Software: SaaS Is Morphing for AI Agents
hook: Enterprise software is quietly losing its head: AI agents are taking over as the primary users, reshaping SaaS architecture.
partner_takeaway: AI agents are driving a shift towards headless architectures and agentic workflows, fundamentally changing SaaS design and usage.
reasoning: The rise of agentic workflows is a macro trend that will affect nearly every industry. Readers need to understand how this changes the market and their own product strategies.

title: Shipping My First AI Tool: Lessons from Production
hook: I still remember the rush—and anxiety—of pushing my first AI-powered tool live for real users.
partner_takeaway: Shipping an AI tool taught me to prioritize user feedback loops and robust error handling from day one.
reasoning: Personal stories of building and shipping resonate with audiences seeking practical guidance. This case-study offers actionable lessons from lived experience, filling the 'building in public' track.

title: A Workflow I Keep Repeating: AI-Powered Prioritization
hook: Whenever my task list explodes, I fall back on an AI-driven prioritization pattern that rarely lets me down.
partner_takeaway: You can use AI to quickly sort and prioritize tasks, but the most common mistake is failing to validate AI output against real constraints.
reasoning: Readers crave repeatable, tactical patterns. Sharing a personal workflow gives them something to try immediately and avoids generic advice.

title: Tech Workforce Divide: Burnout or Breakthrough?
hook: In 2026, tech workers are split: some thrive with AI, others are burning out faster than ever.
partner_takeaway: Leaders must recognize rising burnout among tech workers and adapt management strategies to support both thriving and struggling teams.
reasoning: The annual AI sentiment survey is timely and relevant. Addressing workforce splits and burnout gives readers actionable context for managing their own teams.
